clusters.neutral_headline: Joe Kent Resigns Over Iran War

items.title: What to know about Joe Kent, Trump counterterrorism chief who resigned
items.standfirst: In a resignation letter, Joe Kent said Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation," and he asserted that "we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby."
items.content: In a resignation letter, Joe Kent said Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation," and he asserted that "we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby."

items.content: President Trump's director of the National Counterterrorism Center, Joe Kent, announced his immediate resignation Tuesday, citing the administration's decision to intervene in Iran.

items.standfirst: The head of the US's National Counterterrorism Center, Joe Kent, resigned from his post on Tuesday in opposition to the Trump administration's war on Iran. The former staunch Trump supporter said he could not support the war as "Iran posed no imminent threat" to the US.

items.title: US intelligence chief struggles to avoid contradicting Trump on Iran war
items.standfirst: Tulsi Gabbard refuses to say whether Tehran posed an ‘imminent nuclear threat’
items.content: Tulsi Gabbard refuses to say whether Tehran posed an ‘imminent nuclear threat’

items.title: Watch: Ossoff grills Gabbard on whether Iran poses an "imminent nuclear threat"
items.standfirst: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard told Sen. Jon Ossoff that the intelligence community assessed Iran's nuclear enrichment program was "obliterated" in the 2025 strikes. Ossoff pressed Gabbard about whether Iran posed as an "imminent nuclear threat."
items.content: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard told Sen. Jon Ossoff that the intelligence community assessed Iran's nuclear enrichment program was "obliterated" in the 2025 strikes. Ossoff pressed Gabbard about whether Iran posed as an "imminent nuclear threat."

items.title: Gabbard and Ratcliffe questioned about briefing Trump about Strait of Hormuz closure
items.standfirst: Sen. Mark Kelly questioned Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard and CIA Director John Ratcliffe about briefing President Trump about possibility of Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: Sen. Mark Kelly questioned Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard and CIA Director John Ratcliffe about briefing President Trump about possibility of Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Watch: Gabbard faces scrutiny over Iran nuclear intelligence claims
items.standfirst: The US Director of National Intelligence, Tulsi Gabbard, was questioned over what Democrats perceive as a discrepancy between White House and intelligence community claims.
items.content: The US Director of National Intelligence, Tulsi Gabbard, was questioned over what Democrats perceive as a discrepancy between White House and intelligence community claims.

items.title: Top intelligence officials' testimonies raise concerns over Trump's justification for Iran war
items.standfirst: Top U.S. intelligence officials testified before the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence about worldwide threats. Senators pressed Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard about intelligence assessment and what was shared with President Trump before U.S.-Israeli conducted strikes against Iran. CBS News' Taurean Small has more.
items.content: Top U.S. intelligence officials testified before the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence about worldwide threats. Senators pressed Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard about intelligence assessment and what was shared with President Trump before U.S.-Israeli conducted strikes against Iran. CBS News' Taurean Small has more.

ai_summary: Tulsi Gabbard was questioned about Iran's nuclear threat
Gabbard refused to say if Iran posed an imminent threat
The intelligence community assessed Iran's nuclear program
Gabbard was pressed about briefing President Trump
The testimony raised concerns over Trump's justification for war

clusters.title: Prosecutors seek more than seven years in jail for son of Norway’s crown princess
clusters.neutral_headline: Norway Crown Princess Son On Trial

items.title: Prosecutors seek more than seven years in jail for son of Norway’s crown princess
items.standfirst: Marius Borg Høiby accused of 39 offences, but denies the most serious charges of four rapesMarius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, should receive more than seven years in prison if he is found guilty of 39 offences, including four rapes and assaults, according to prosecutors.On Wednesday, the penultimate day of the more than six-week-long trial at Oslo district court, the prosecution said it believed that Høiby was guilty of 39 of the 40 offences with which he was charged, which, as well as rape and domestic abuse, include multiple breaches of restraining orders, assault, drug and driving offences. Continue reading...

Continue reading...
